title: saccoloma kingii
side_effects: Saccoloma kingii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Ingesting any part of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to its sap or oils can cause inflammation, redness, and blistering on the skin. If not handled carefully, Saccoloma kingii can also trigger anaphylaxis, a severe and potentially life-threatening allergic reaction, in rare cases.
